issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
3 from urllib.parse import urlparse
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.

issues.code:
9             # Parse the XML request body using a secure parser
10             tree = ET.parse(self.rfile)
11             root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).

issues.code:
1 import xml.etree.ElementTree as ET
2 from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
3 from urllib.parse import urlparse
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree with the equivalent defusedxml package, or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called.
issues.judge_reason: Importing xml.etree.ElementTree without defusedxml is a real risk. This is the same underlying vulnerability as B314 and py/xml-bomb, but at the import level.

issues.code:
9             # Parse the XML request body using a secure parser
10             tree = ET.parse(self.rfile)
11             root = tree.getroot()
issues.description: Using xml.etree.ElementTree.parse to parse untrusted XML data is known to be vulnerable to XML attacks. Replace xml.etree.ElementTree.parse with its defusedxml equivalent function or make sure defusedxml.defuse_stdlib() is called
issues.judge_reason: ET.parse is used without defusedxml or entity expansion protection. The comment 'secure parser' is misleading; no actual security measures are applied. This is a real XXE vulnerability.

duplicate_findings.description: XML parsing depends on a [user-provided value](1) without guarding against uncontrolled entity expansion.
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: Same root cause as B314: ET.parse allows entity expansion (billion laughs attack). No guard against it.

duplicate_findings.description: This path depends on a [user-provided value](1).
duplicate_findings.judge_reason: The path injection is not directly on line 10 (ET.parse) but the user-controlled profile_path is later used in open() without proper path restriction that only checks startswith and '..' but does not normalize the path, allowing bypasses like symlinks or encoded sequences. The SAST finding line reference may be imprecise; the real sink is the open() call. Marking UNCERTAIN because the evidence is indirect but the risk is real.
